NKJV.1: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, behold, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem,
NKJV.2: saying, “Where is He who has been born King of the Jews? For we have seen His star in the East and have come to worship Him.”
NKJV.3: When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
NKJV.4: And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born.
NKJV.5: So they said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for thus it is written by the prophet:
NKJV.6: ‘But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, Are not the least among the rulers of Judah; For out of you shall come a Ruler Who will shepherd My people Israel.’ ”
NKJV.7: Then Herod, when he had secretly called the wise men, determined from them what time the star appeared.
NKJV.8: And he sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and search carefully for the young Child, and when you have found Him, bring back word to me, that I may come and worship Him also.”
NKJV.9: When they heard the king, they departed; and behold, the star which they had seen in the East went before them, till it came and stood over where the young Child was.
NKJV.10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.
NKJV.11: And when they had come into the house, they saw the young Child with Mary His mother, and fell down and worshiped Him. And when they had opened their treasures, they presented gifts to Him: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
NKJV.12: Then, being divinely warned in a dream that they should not return to Herod, they departed for their own country another way.
NKJV.13: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Arise, take the young Child and His mother, flee to Egypt, and stay there until I bring you word; for Herod will seek the young Child to destroy Him.”
NKJV.14: When he arose, he took the young Child and His mother by night and departed for Egypt,
NKJV.15: and was there until the death of Herod, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called My Son.”
NKJV.16: Then Herod, when he saw that he was deceived by the wise men, was exceedingly angry; and he sent forth and put to death all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all its districts, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had determined from the wise men.
NKJV.17: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying:
NKJV.18: “A voice was heard in Ramah, Lamentation, weeping, and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, Refusing to be comforted, Because they are no more.”
NKJV.19: Now when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
NKJV.20: saying, “Arise, take the young Child and His mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who sought the young Child’s life are dead.”
NKJV.21: Then he arose, took the young Child and His mother, and came into the land of Israel.
NKJV.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea instead of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And being warned by God in a dream, he turned aside into the region of Galilee.
NKJV.23: And he came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophets, “He shall be called a Nazarene.”

ESV.1: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, behold, wise men  from the east came to Jerusalem,
ESV.2: saying, “Where is he who has been born king of the Jews? For we saw his star when it rose  and have come to worship him.”
ESV.3: When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him;
ESV.4: and assembling all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born.
ESV.5: They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it is written by the prophet:
ESV.6: “‘And you, O Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”
ESV.7: Then Herod summoned the wise men secretly and ascertained from them what time the star had appeared.
ESV.8: And he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, “Go and search diligently for the child, and when you have found him, bring me word, that I too may come and worship him.”
ESV.9: After listening to the king, they went on their way. And behold, the star that they had seen when it rose went before them until it came to rest over the place where the child was.
ESV.10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
ESV.11: And going into the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother, and they fell down and worshiped him. Then, opening their treasures, they offered him gifts, gold and frankincense and myrrh.
ESV.12: And being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed to their own country by another way.
ESV.13: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Rise, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.”
ESV.14: And he rose and took the child and his mother by night and departed to Egypt
ESV.15: and remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet, “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
ESV.16: Then Herod, when he saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, became furious, and he sent and killed all the male children in Bethlehem and in all that region who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had ascertained from the wise men.
ESV.17: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by the prophet Jeremiah:
ESV.18: “A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and loud lamentation, Rachel weeping for her children; she refused to be comforted, because they are no more.”
ESV.19: But when Herod died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
ESV.20: saying, “Rise, take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel, for those who sought the child's life are dead.”
ESV.21: And he rose and took the child and his mother and went to the land of Israel.
ESV.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there, and being warned in a dream he withdrew to the district of Galilee.
ESV.23: And he went and lived in a city called Nazareth, so that what was spoken by the prophets might be fulfilled, that he would be called a Nazarene.

NASB.1: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem, saying,
NASB.2: 'Where is He who has been born King of the Jews? For we saw His star in the east and have come to worship Him.'
NASB.3: When Herod the king heard [this], he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
NASB.4: Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
NASB.5: They said to him, 'In Bethlehem of Judea; for this is what has been written by the prophet:
NASB.6: 'AND YOU, BETHLEHEM, LAND OF JUDAH, ARE BY NO MEANS LEAST AMONG THE LEADERS OF JUDAH; FOR OUT OF YOU SHALL COME FORTH A RULER WHO WILL SHEPHERD MY PEOPLE ISRAEL.''
NASB.7: Then Herod secretly called the magi and determined from them the exact time the star appeared.
NASB.8: And he sent them to Bethlehem and said, 'Go and search carefully for the Child; and when you have found [Him], report to me, so that I too may come and worship Him.'
NASB.9: After hearing the king, they went their way; and the star, which they had seen in the east, went on before them until it came and stood over [the place] where the Child was.
NASB.10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
NASB.11: After coming into the house they saw the Child with Mary His mother; and they fell to the ground and worshiped Him. Then, opening their treasures, they presented to Him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
NASB.12: And having been warned [by God] in a dream not to return to Herod, the magi left for their own country by another way.
NASB.13: Now when they had gone, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, 'Get up! Take the Child and His mother and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod is going to search for the Child to destroy Him.'
NASB.14: So Joseph got up and took the Child and His mother while it was still night, and left for Egypt.
NASB.15: He remained there until the death of Herod. [This was] to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet: 'OUT OF EGYPT I CALLED MY SON.'
NASB.16: Then when Herod saw that he had been tricked by the magi, he became very enraged, and sent and slew all the male children who were in Bethlehem and all its vicinity, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had determined from the magi.
NASB.17: Then what had been spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
NASB.18: 'A VOICE WAS HEARD IN RAMAH, WEEPING AND GREAT MOURNING, RACHEL WEEPING FOR HER CHILDREN; AND SHE REFUSED TO BE COMFORTED, BECAUSE THEY WERE NO MORE.'
NASB.19: But when Herod died, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, and said,
NASB.20: 'Get up, take the Child and His mother, and go into the land of Israel; for those who sought the Child's life are dead.'
NASB.21: So Joseph got up, took the Child and His mother, and came into the land of Israel.
NASB.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Then after being warned [by God] in a dream, he left for the regions of Galilee,
NASB.23: and came and lived in a city called Nazareth. [This was] to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets: 'He shall be called a Nazarene.'

NLT.1: Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the reign of King Herod. About that time some wise men from eastern lands arrived in Jerusalem, asking,
NLT.2: “Where is the newborn king of the Jews? We saw his star as it rose, and we have come to worship him.”
NLT.3: King Herod was deeply disturbed when he heard this, as was everyone in Jerusalem.
NLT.4: He called a meeting of the leading priests and teachers of religious law and asked, “Where is the Messiah supposed to be born?”
NLT.5: “In Bethlehem in Judea,” they said, “for this is what the prophet wrote:
NLT.6:
‘And you, O Bethlehem in the land of Judah,
are not least among the ruling cities of Judah,
for a ruler will come from you
who will be the shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
NLT.7: Then Herod called for a private meeting with the wise men, and he learned from them the time when the star first appeared.
NLT.8: Then he told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. And when you find him, come back and tell me so that I can go and worship him, too!”
NLT.9: After this interview the wise men went their way. And the star they had seen in the east guided them to Bethlehem. It went ahead of them and stopped over the place where the child was.
NLT.10: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy!
NLT.11: They entered the house and saw the child with his mother, Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasure chests and gave him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
NLT.12: When it was time to leave, they returned to their own country by another route, for God had warned them in a dream not to return to Herod.
NLT.13: After the wise men were gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. “Get up! Flee to Egypt with the child and his mother,” the angel said. “Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is going to search for the child to kill him.”
NLT.14: That night Joseph left for Egypt with the child and Mary, his mother,
NLT.15: and they stayed there until Herod’s death. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: “I called my Son out of Egypt.”
NLT.16: Herod was furious when he realized that the wise men had outwitted him. He sent soldiers to kill all the boys in and around Bethlehem who were two years old and under, based on the wise men’s report of the star’s first appearance.
NLT.17: Herod’s brutal action fulfilled what God had spoken through the prophet Jeremiah:
NLT.18:
“A cry was heard in Ramah —
weeping and great mourning.
Rachel weeps for her children,
refusing to be comforted,
for they are dead.”
NLT.19: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
NLT.20: “Get up!” the angel said. “Take the child and his mother back to the land of Israel, because those who were trying to kill the child are dead.”
NLT.21: So Joseph got up and returned to the land of Israel with Jesus and his mother.
NLT.22: But when he learned that the new ruler of Judea was Herod’s son Archelaus, he was afraid to go there. Then, after being warned in a dream, he left for the region of Galilee.
NLT.23: So the family went and lived in a town called Nazareth. This fulfilled what the prophets had said: “He will be called a Nazarene.”

WEB.1: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, behold, wise men  from the east came to Jerusalem, saying,
WEB.2: “Where is he who is born King of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east, and have come to worship him.”
WEB.3: When King Herod heard it, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
WEB.4: Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ would be born.
WEB.5: They said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is written through the prophet,
WEB.6: ‘You Bethlehem, land of Judah, are in no way least among the princes of Judah: for out of you shall come a governor, who shall shepherd my people, Israel.’”
WEB.7: Then Herod secretly called the wise men, and learned from them exactly what time the star appeared.
WEB.8: He sent them to Bethlehem, and said, “Go and search diligently for the young child. When you have found him, bring me word, so that I also may come and worship him.”
WEB.9: They, having heard the king, went their way; and behold, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, until it came and stood over where the young child was.
WEB.10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.
WEB.11: They came into the house and saw the young child with Mary, his mother, and they fell down and worshiped him. Opening their treasures, they offered to him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
WEB.12: Being warned in a dream that they shouldn’t return to Herod, they went back to their own country another way.
WEB.13: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and stay there until I tell you, for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.”
WEB.14: He arose and took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt,
WEB.15: and was there until the death of Herod; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
WEB.16: Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly angry, and sent out, and killed all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding countryside, from two years old and under, according to the exact time which he had learned from the wise men.
WEB.17: Then that which was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying,
WEB.18: “A voice was heard in Ramah, lamentation, weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children; she wouldn’t be comforted, because they are no more.”
WEB.19: But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, saying,
WEB.20: “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those who sought the young child’s life are dead.”
WEB.21: He arose and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.
WEB.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in the place of his father, Herod, he was afraid to go there. Being warned in a dream, he withdrew into the region of Galilee,
WEB.23: and came and lived in a city called Nazareth; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets: “He will be called a Nazarene.”

CSB17.1: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, wise men from the east arrived in Jerusalem, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.2: saying, “Where is he who has been born king of the Jews? <sup>ⓜ</sup> For we saw his star at its rising <sup>ⓝ</sup> and have come to worship him.” <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.3: When King Herod heard this, he was deeply disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.
CSB17.4: So he assembled all the chief priests and scribes <sup>ⓐ</sup> of the people and asked them where the Christ would be born.
CSB17.5: “In Bethlehem of Judea,” they told him, “because this is what was written by the prophet:
CSB17.6:
And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
are by no means least among the rulers of Judah:
Because out of you will come a ruler
who will shepherd my people Israel.” [2] ⓑ
CSB17.7: Then Herod secretly summoned the wise men and asked them the exact time the star appeared.
CSB17.8: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and search carefully for the child. When you find him, report back to me so that I too can go and worship him.” <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.9: After hearing the king, they went on their way. And there it was — the star they had seen at its rising. It led them until it came and stopped above the place where the child was.
CSB17.10: When they saw the star, they were overwhelmed with joy.
CSB17.11: Entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother, and falling to their knees, they worshiped him. <sup>[4]</sup> Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.12: And being warned <sup>ⓔ</sup> in a dream not to go back to Herod, they returned to their own country by another route.
CSB17.13: After they were gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Get up! Take the child and his mother, flee to Egypt, and stay there until I tell you. For Herod is about to search for the child to kill him.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.14: So he got up, took the child and his mother during the night, and escaped to Egypt.
CSB17.15: He stayed there until Herod’s death, so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet might be fulfilled: Out of Egypt I called my Son. [5] ⓖ
CSB17.16: Then Herod, when he realized that he had been outwitted by the wise men, flew into a rage. He gave orders to massacre all the boys in and around Bethlehem who were two years old and under, in keeping with the time he had learned from the wise men. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: Then what was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
CSB17.18:
A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping, [6] and great mourning,
Rachel weeping for her children;
and she refused to be consoled,
because they are no more. [7] ⓘ
CSB17.19: After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.20: saying, “Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, because those who intended to kill the child are dead.”
CSB17.21: So he got up, took the child and his mother, and entered the land of Israel.
CSB17.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And being warned in a dream, he withdrew to the region of Galilee. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.23: Then he went and settled in a town called Nazareth to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets, that he would be called a Nazarene. <sup>ⓛ</sup>

AMP.1: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king (Herod the Great), magi (wise men) from the east came to Jerusalem, asking,
AMP.2: "Where is He who has been born King of the Jews? For we have seen His star in the east and have come to worship Him."
AMP.3: When Herod the king heard this, he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.
AMP.4: So he called together all the chief priests and scribes of the people and [anxiously] asked them where the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) was to be born.
AMP.5: They replied to him, "In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what has been written by the prophet [Micah]:
AMP.6:
'And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
are not in any way least among the leaders of Judah;
For from you shall come a Ruler
Who will shepherd My people Israel.'"
AMP.7: Then Herod secretly sent for the magi and learned from them the [exact] time the star [had first] appeared.
AMP.8: Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, "Go and search carefully for the Child; and when you have found Him, report to me, so that I too may come and worship Him."
AMP.9: After hearing the king, they went their way; and behold, the star, which they had seen in the east, went on before them [continually leading the way] until it came and stood over the place where the young Child was.
AMP.10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
AMP.11: And after entering the house, they saw the Child with Mary His mother; and they fell down and worshiped Him. Then, after opening their treasure chests, they presented to Him gifts [fit for a king, gifts] of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
AMP.12: And having been warned [by God] in a dream not to go back to Herod, the magi left for their own country by another way.
AMP.13: Now when they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, "Get up! Take the Child and His mother and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod intends to search for the Child in order to destroy Him."
AMP.14: So Joseph got up and took the Child and His mother while it was still night, and left for Egypt.
AMP.15: He remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet [Hosea]: "Out of Egypt I called My Son."
AMP.16: Then Herod, when he realized that he had been tricked by the magi, was extremely angry, and he sent [soldiers] and put to death all the male children in Bethlehem and in all that area who were two years old and under, according to the date which he had learned from the magi.
AMP.17: Then what had been spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
AMP.18:
"A voice was heard inRamah,
Weeping and great mourning,
Rachel weeping for her children;
She refused to be comforted,
Because they were no more."
AMP.19: But when Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, and said,
AMP.20: "Get up! Take the Child and His mother, and go to the land of Israel; for those who sought the Child's life are dead."
AMP.21: Then Joseph got up, and took the Child and His mother, and came into the land of Israel.
AMP.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod [the Great], he was afraid to go there. Then being warned by God in a dream, he left for the region of Galilee,
AMP.23: and went and settled in a city called Nazareth. This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophets: "He shall be called a Nazarene."

MSG.1: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem village, Judah territory—this was during Herod's kingship—a band of scholars arrived in Jerusalem from the East.
MSG.2: They asked around, "Where can we find and pay homage to the newborn King of the Jews? We observed a star in the eastern sky that signaled his birth. We're on pilgrimage to worship him."
MSG.3: When word of their inquiry got to Herod, he was terrified—and not Herod alone, but most of Jerusalem as well.
MSG.4: Herod lost no time. He gathered all the high priests and religion scholars in the city together and asked, "Where is the Messiah supposed to be born?"
MSG.5: They told him, "Bethlehem, Judah territory. The prophet Micah wrote it plainly:
MSG.6: It's you, Bethlehem, in Judah's land, no longer bringing up the rear. From you will come the leader who will shepherd-rule my people, my Israel."
MSG.7: Herod then arranged a secret meeting with the scholars from the East. Pretending to be as devout as they were, he got them to tell him exactly when the birth-announcement star appeared.
MSG.8: Then he told them the prophecy about Bethlehem, and said, "Go find this child. Leave no stone unturned. As soon as you find him, send word and I'll join you at once in your worship."
MSG.9: Instructed by the king, they set off. Then the star appeared again, the same star they had seen in the eastern skies. It led them on until it hovered over the place of the child.
MSG.10: They could hardly contain themselves: They were in the right place! They had arrived at the right time!
MSG.11: They entered the house and saw the child in the arms of Mary, his mother. Overcome, they kneeled and worshiped him. Then they opened their luggage and presented gifts: gold, frankincense, myrrh.
MSG.12: In a dream, they were warned not to report back to Herod. So they worked out another route, left the territory without being seen, and returned to their own country.
MSG.13: After the scholars were gone, God's angel showed up again in Joseph's dream and commanded, "Get up. Take the child and his mother and flee to Egypt. Stay until further notice. Herod is on the hunt for this child, and wants to kill him."
MSG.14: Joseph obeyed. He got up, took the child and his mother under cover of darkness. They were out of town and well on their way by daylight.
MSG.15: They lived in Egypt until Herod's death. This Egyptian exile fulfilled what Hosea had preached: "I called my son out of Egypt."
MSG.16: Herod, when he realized that the scholars had tricked him, flew into a rage. He commanded the murder of every little boy two years old and under who lived in Bethlehem and its surrounding hills. (He determined that age from information he'd gotten from the scholars.)
MSG.17: That's when Jeremiah's sermon was fulfilled:
MSG.18: A sound was heard in Ramah, weeping and much lament. Rachel weeping for her children, Rachel refusing all solace, Her children gone, dead and buried.
MSG.19: Later, when Herod died, God's angel appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt:
MSG.20: "Up, take the child and his mother and return to Israel. All those out to murder the child are dead."
MSG.21: Joseph obeyed. He got up, took the child and his mother, and reentered Israel.
MSG.22: When he heard, though, that Archelaus had succeeded his father, Herod, as king in Judea, he was afraid to go there. But then Joseph was directed in a dream to go to the hills of Galilee.
MSG.23: On arrival, he settled in the village of Nazareth. This move was a fulfillment of the prophetic words, "He shall be called a Nazarene."

NIV.1:
The Visit of the Magi
After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east came to Jerusalem
NIV.2: and asked, “Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
NIV.3: When King Herod heard this he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.
NIV.4: When he had called together all the people’s chief priests and teachers of the law, he asked them where the Christ was to be born.
NIV.5: “In Bethlehem in Judea,” they replied, “for this is what the prophet has written:
NIV.6:
“‘But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
are by no means least among the rulers of Judah;
for out of you will come a ruler
who will be the shepherd of my people Israel.’”
NIV.7: Then Herod called the Magi secretly and found out from them the exact time the star had appeared.
NIV.8: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and make a careful search for the child. As soon as you find him, report to me, so that I too may go and worship him.”
NIV.9: After they had heard the king, they went on their way, and the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
NIV.10: When they saw the star, they were overjoyed.
NIV.11: On coming to the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold and of incense and of myrrh.
NIV.12: And having been warned in a dream not to go back to Herod, they returned to their country by another route.
NIV.13:
The Escape to Egypt
When they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. “Get up,” he said, “take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him.”
NIV.14: So he got up, took the child and his mother during the night and left for Egypt,
NIV.15: where he stayed until the death of Herod. And so was fulfilled what the Lord had said through the prophet: “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
NIV.16: When Herod realized that he had been outwitted by the Magi, he was furious, and he gave orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity who were two years old and under, in accordance with the time he had learned from the Magi.
NIV.17: Then what was said through the prophet Jeremiah was fulfilled:
NIV.18:
“A voice is heard in Ramah,
weeping and great mourning,
Rachel weeping for her children
and refusing to be comforted,
because they are no more.”
NIV.19:
The Return to Nazareth
After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt
NIV.20: and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel, for those who were trying to take the child’s life are dead.”
NIV.21: So he got up, took the child and his mother and went to the land of Israel.
NIV.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee,
NIV.23: and he went and lived in a town called Nazareth. So was fulfilled what was said through the prophets: “He will be called a Nazarene.”

NRSVCE.1: In the time of King Herod, after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem,
NRSVCE.2: asking, “Where is the child who has been born king of the Jews? For we observed his star at its rising, and have come to pay him homage.”
NRSVCE.3: When King Herod heard this, he was frightened, and all Jerusalem with him;
NRSVCE.4: and calling together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
NRSVCE.5: They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for so it has been written by the prophet:
NRSVCE.6:
‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
are by no means least among the rulers of Judah;
for from you shall come a ruler
who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”
NRSVCE.7: Then Herod secretly called for the wise men and learned from them the exact time when the star had appeared.
NRSVCE.8: Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, “Go and search diligently for the child; and when you have found him, bring me word so that I may also go and pay him homage.”
NRSVCE.9: When they had heard the king, they set out; and there, ahead of them, went the star that they had seen at its rising, until it stopped over the place where the child was.
NRSVCE.10: When they saw that the star had stopped, they were overwhelmed with joy.
NRSVCE.11: On entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother; and they knelt down and paid him homage. Then, opening their treasure chests, they offered him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
NRSVCE.12: And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they left for their own country by another road.
NRSVCE.13: Now after they had left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.”
NRSVCE.14: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother by night, and went to Egypt,
NRSVCE.15: and remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet, “Out of Egypt I have called my son.”
NRSVCE.16: When Herod saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, he was infuriated, and he sent and killed all the children in and around Bethlehem who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had learned from the wise men.
NRSVCE.17: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken through the prophet Jeremiah:
NRSVCE.18:
“A voice was heard in Ramah,
wailing and loud lamentation,
Rachel weeping for her children;
she refused to be consoled, because they are no more.”
NRSVCE.19: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord suddenly appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt and said,
NRSVCE.20: “Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were seeking the child’s life are dead.”
NRSVCE.21: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
NRSVCE.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And after being warned in a dream, he went away to the district of Galilee.
NRSVCE.23: There he made his home in a town called Nazareth, so that what had been spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled, “He will be called a Nazorean.”

NET.1: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, in the time of King Herod, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem
NET.2: saying, “Where is the one who is born king of the Jews? For we saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him.”
NET.3: When King Herod heard this he was alarmed, and all Jerusalem with him.
NET.4: After assembling all the chief priests and experts in the law, he asked them where the Christ was to be born.
NET.5: “In Bethlehem of Judea,” they said, “for it is written this way by the prophet:
NET.6: ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are in no way least among the rulers of Judah, for out of you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”
NET.7: Then Herod privately summoned the wise men and determined from them when the star had appeared.
NET.8: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and look carefully for the child. When you find him, inform me so that I can go and worship him as well.”
NET.9: After listening to the king they left, and once again the star they saw when it rose led them until it stopped above the place where the child was.
NET.10: When they saw the star they shouted joyfully.
NET.11: As they came into the house and saw the child with Mary his mother, they bowed down and worshiped him. They opened their treasure boxes and gave him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
NET.12: After being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back by another route to their own country.
NET.13: After they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother and flee to Egypt, and stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to look for the child to kill him.”
NET.14: Then he got up, took the child and his mother during the night, and went to Egypt.
NET.15: He stayed there until Herod died. In this way what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet was fulfilled: “I called my Son out of Egypt.”
NET.16: When Herod saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, he became enraged. He sent men to kill all the children in Bethlehem and throughout the surrounding region from the age of two and under, according to the time he had learned from the wise men.
NET.17: Then what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled:
NET.18: “A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and loud wailing, Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were gone.”
NET.19: After Herod had died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt
NET.20: saying, “Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were seeking the child’s life are dead.”
NET.21: So he got up and took the child and his mother and returned to the land of Israel.
NET.22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. After being warned in a dream, he went to the regions of Galilee.
NET.23: He came to a town called Nazareth and lived there. Then what had been spoken by the prophets was fulfilled, that Jesus would be called a Nazarene.
